title: Data Protection Risk Matrix
description: A standard 5x5 risk matrix for assessing data protection risks under the Malaysia PDPA 2010. Risk scores are calculated by multiplying likelihood (1-5) by impact (1-5) to produce a score from 1 to 25, mapped to four risk bands with prescribed actions.

bands.description: Risk is within acceptable tolerance. Monitor through standard review cycles. No additional treatment required unless cost-effective measures are available.
bands.pdpaNote: Even low-rated risks must comply with mandatory PDPA requirements. Low rating does not excuse non-compliance with any Principle.

bands.description: Risk requires active management. Implement controls from the PDPA Controls Library to reduce risk to acceptable levels. Treatment plans should be established within 90 days.
bands.escalation: Data Protection Officer and department head
bands.pdpaNote: Ensure all applicable PDPA controls are implemented. Review against relevant codes of practice and guidelines.

bands.description: Risk exceeds acceptable tolerance and requires priority treatment. Implement additional controls immediately. Treatment plans must be established within 30 days with interim measures in place.
bands.escalation: Senior management and DPO; Board Risk Committee if involving sensitive personal data
bands.pdpaNote: May trigger DPIA requirement under s137. Consider whether JPDP consultation is required. Ensure breach notification readiness under s12B.

bands.description: Risk is at an unacceptable level. Requires immediate executive intervention. Processing activity may need to be suspended until risk is reduced. Emergency treatment plan within 7 days.
bands.reviewFrequency: Weekly until reduced to High or below
bands.escalation: Board of Directors, CEO, DPO, and legal counsel. Consider proactive engagement with JPDP.
bands.pdpaNote: DPIA is almost certainly required under s137. Consider suspending the processing activity. Assess whether Commissioner notification is needed. Review officer liability exposure under s133A.
